title: Flow Control
description: Show, For, Switch, Match, and ErrorBoundary decide which components run based on reactive conditions.
one_tree_intro: The uptime monitor's layout uses every flow component at once. <0>ErrorBoundary</0> wraps a <1>For</1> that mounts one probe per target, <2>Show</2> holds the status page back until the first sweep lands, and a <3>Switch</3> of <4>Match</4> branches picks a banner from the overall status.
info_accessor_conditions: Flow components take accessor functions such as <0>() => value</0> for their conditions. The condition re-evaluates on its own when signals change, without re-running the parent component.
switch_states_intro: A <0>Switch</0> reads straight from a resource. Each page in the page writer exposes a <1>state</1> accessor, and the Match branches render the writing, ready, and failed views as the handler advances through them.
